hard.next_step: 6/29 - Invited to Horizon 2026. Pending response. 6/22 - Reviewed the Final business case draft; presenting to Chief Supply Officer and VP of Procurement Mid July; follow up End of July.
hard.ais_why: AI importance rated Neutral; no Merlin/ANA/Agentic tagged. Genuine IT-led AI roadmap exploration is happening (Rafal's team is the pilot group), but AI is not yet a must-have on this P2P deal.
hard.competitor: Undisclosed multi-vendor evaluation (named after funding); status quo (manual/unmanaged process on Oracle ERP)

north_star_verdict.headline: Genuinely healthy, buyer-engaged deal with a co-built conservative business case, but the 19 Aug close is not credible: a mid-July CSCO funding gate Zycus cannot attend comes first, then a multi-vendor selection, then contracting and Oracle integration.
north_star_verdict.math: Today 06 Jul 2026; close 19 Aug 2026 = 44 days out. In Formal Evaluation since 25 Nov 2025 (~7.4 months). Close date pushed five times (19 Mar -> 30 Apr -> 20 May -> 18 Jun -> 30 Jun -> 30 Jul -> 19 Aug, latest slip on 30 Jun). Remaining path per 18 Jun call: pre-run with VP Procurement, present to CSCO Oksana mid-July for funding, THEN run vendor selection, THEN contracting/InfoSec/Oracle integration. None of that completes in 44 days; a Q3/Q4 2026 close is realistic.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline / Upside with a Q3-Q4 2026 close date; not an August close.
evidence: Jacquelyn Guerrero, 18 Jun 2026: 'we are trying to prepare to do a presentation to her in July now.'
Jacquelyn Guerrero, 18 Jun 2026: 'we've got multiple vendors that we're looking at... after we make that initial case for funding, then it's gonna be about which vendor is right for Amy's.'
SF NextStep (29 Jun 2026): final business case draft reviewed 6/22; presenting to Chief Supply Officer and VP of Procurement mid-July; follow up end of July.
SF field history (30 Jun 2026): CloseDate 30 Jul -> 19 Aug 2026 (by Steve Ovadje) - a fifth slip.

metrics.narrative: The value case is quantified and co-built, which is the strongest element of this deal. On 31 Mar 2026 Jordan Weimer (Zycus SC) framed ~$208M total spend, ~50% managed today targeting 90%, ~$14.5M unmanaged indirect spend for tail-spend automation, a 3% baseline savings rate plus 1-7% from autonomous sourcing, and one point of managed spend worth roughly $1M. By the 18 Jun final-draft review the conservative Scope A (P2P) model showed an ~8-month payback and 5-7x five-year ROI, with a 50% realization-rate assumption. The buyer stress-tested the math themselves: Rafal Prylinski explicitly asked to keep it conservative ('we don't want to overcommit... then we're gonna put big targets on our back'), and Jacquelyn Guerrero endorsed the conservative approach on 18 Jun.
sources: Avoma Business Case Review 1st Draft 31 Mar 2026 (Jordan Weimer, Rafal Prylinski)
Avoma Final Draft 18 Jun 2026 (Jordan Weimer, Jacquelyn Guerrero)

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is named by the champion but has never been met by Zycus. Jacquelyn Guerrero, 18 Jun: 'Our chief supply chain officer, Oksana, will be her in July... we're gonna do a prerun with our VP of procurement as soon as we've got the numbers pulled together, and then we'll get on her calendar.' Oksana (Chief Supply Chain Officer) controls the funding decision. When Steve Ovadje offered to be in the room, Jacquelyn declined: 'she is most receptive to her internal team.' So the funding pitch is champion-carried by design; the path to power is NOT a cold Zycus approach to Oksana but making the champions unbeatable in a room Zycus cannot enter, and offering a VP-to-EB touch only through the champion. Note: Oksana is buyer-named on a call, not a Salesforce contact, so she is unverified as a Salesforce record.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are explicit and repeatedly stated. First, a conservative, defensible ROI executives can stand behind (Mike McCarthy, 31 Mar: 'a good, clean, defensible business case'; Rafal, 18 Jun: 'we don't want to overcommit... big targets on our back'). Second, a 2-3 year ROI target the conservative model already clears (Jordan, 18 Jun: 'two to three year ROI was your target... we're well under that'). Third, Oracle ERP coexistence: Jacquelyn pressed hard on whether Zycus can support 3-way PO/receipt/invoice matching staying in Oracle while Zycus drives requisition-to-PO. Fourth, AI/automation appetite to cut repetitive work and headcount. The Scope A (P2P) vs Scope B (full source-to-pay + iContract/autonomous sourcing) choice remains open and is the buyer's stated next internal deliberation.
sources: Avoma Final Draft 18 Jun 2026 (Jordan Weimer, Jacquelyn Guerrero)

decision_process.narrative: The process is clear and sequenced, read directly from the call and NextStep. Per 18 Jun: champions do a pre-run with the VP of Procurement, then present to CSCO Oksana mid-July to secure funding, THEN move to vendor selection ('once we have that conversation... which vendor is right for Amy's'), THEN an AP-team follow-up to finalize Oracle PO/invoice matching. Champions lead every internal step with the EB held in reserve. A late-April structural change (Rafal now owns operations/services plus MRO, and a new CEO announced) caused the spring delays but, per Jacquelyn, 'further cements the need for P2P.'

paper_process.narrative: Contracting is downstream of funding and explicitly unowned. Jacquelyn, 18 Jun: 'we're still trying to figure out what we're doing with the contracting, to be honest.' No MSA, SOW, legal review, or InfoSec sequence has started, and none should be expected before the mid-July funding gate and the subsequent vendor selection. A signed NDA is in place (Steve sent the fully executed NDA on 20 Mar 2026). A mutual close/paper plan tied to the post-funding selection date would create the missing visibility.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is specific and owner-attributed. Rafal Prylinski, 31 Mar: indirect spend is largely uncontracted, MRO carries 350+ vendors, and the team wants to 'eliminate all these repetitive tasks.' Jacquelyn detailed manual MRO intake ('someone might leave you a Post-it... someone might send you an email') and a manual AP review process where the team reviews nearly everything. Only ~25% of ops/services and MRO spend is managed today (vs 100% on the $60M enterprise bucket), leaving ~75% of those categories exposed. The June restructure putting ops/services and MRO under Rafal, plus the VP-of-IT AI roadmap that designates procurement as the pilot group, are the compelling context.

champion.narrative: Two engaged, active champions drive this deal: Jacquelyn Guerrero (Director, Packaging and Indirect Materials) and Rafal Prylinski (Manager, Indirect Spend). They co-built the business case, stress-tested the numbers, and will personally present to the CSCO and VP of Procurement mid-July. Engagement is recent and consistent (18 Jun). The gap is structural, not motivational: their advocacy is untested at the executive level and Zycus is not in the funding room, so champion enablement (an executive-ready one-pager and rehearsal) is the lever, not bypass. Rebecah Samo (Enterprise Purchasing Manager, leading the contract-repository project) is a lighter, warm third thread.
sources: Avoma Final Draft 18 Jun 2026 (Jacquelyn Guerrero, Rafal Prylinski)

competition.narrative: Competition is real but unnamed. Jacquelyn, 18 Jun: 'we've got multiple vendors that we're looking at... once we have funding, then it's gonna be about which vendor is right for Amy's.' The vendor bake-off is deliberately sequenced AFTER the funding gate, so Zycus has zero visibility into competitor names, pricing, or timing. The practical incumbent is Amy's manual/unmanaged process on Oracle ERP. Zycus leads on relationship and the co-built conservative business case; the win path is to name the field via the champions now, then lock the post-funding window with a staged formal proposal, peer CPG references, and an Oracle 3-way-match integration design.

critical_signals.text: The 18 Jun final business case review landed well: Jacquelyn said Zycus 'delivered what we asked for' with a conservative model and now wants to weigh Scope A (P2P) versus Scope B (full source-to-pay).
A multi-vendor selection is deliberately sequenced after the mid-July funding decision, and Zycus has no names, pricing, or timing on the other vendors.
Chief Supply Chain Officer Oksana is the mid-July funding decision-maker and has never met Zycus, with the pitch carried by the champions after Zycus attendance was declined.
The conservative co-built ROI (~8-month payback, 5-7x over five years) is credible, but the buyer still needs per-module pricing to present internally.
The close date slipped a fifth time to 19 Aug on 30 Jun while the funding gate is not until mid-July, so the forecast date is not credible.

deal_movement.summary: Deal is live and buyer-engaged. Momentum resumed after a spring lull caused by an Amy's restructure and new-CEO announcement; the final business case was reviewed 18 Jun and champions are preparing a mid-July executive funding pitch. Last real two-way touch was Steve's 29 Jun Horizon 2026 invite (rep-initiated, awaiting reply).

items.change: Final business case draft reviewed with champions; buyer endorsed the conservative model and asked to weigh Scope A vs Scope B.
Steve Ovadje sent the business case recap and invited champions to Horizon 2026; response pending.
Close date pushed from 30 Jul to 19 Aug 2026 (fifth slip).
Amy's structural change: Rafal Prylinski took on operations/services plus MRO; new CEO announced late April.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus leads on relationship and a co-built conservative business case, but a live multi-vendor selection is sequenced after the mid-July funding gate. The strongest current threat is the unnamed vendor field Jacquelyn flagged on 18 Jun (no name, price, or timing known); the practical incumbent is Amy's manual/unmanaged process on Oracle ERP. No rival has been named, priced, or timed as of 06 Jul 2026.

best_practice_check.summary: Highest-impact levers to win from Formal Evaluation: arm the champions for a room Zycus cannot enter, uncover the competitive field before the post-funding bake-off, and pre-stage the commercials so the deal can move the moment funding lands.
flags: Equip Jacquelyn and Rafal with an executive-ready one-page ROI summary and a rehearsal before the mid-July CSCO funding pitch, since Zycus attendance was declined.
Uncover the unnamed competing vendors now via the champions, so the post-funding selection is not a blind bake-off.
Pre-stage per-module pricing (Scope A vs Scope B) plus peer CPG references and an Oracle 3-way-match integration design to deploy within days of funding approval.

champion_strength.summary: Two active champions co-built the business case and will personally present to the economic buyer, but their advocacy is untested at the executive level and Zycus is not in the funding room, so champion enablement is the lever, not bypass.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI appetite is real but IT-led and early: Rafal is building an Amy's procurement AI roadmap and designated the team as the pilot group, yet the deal is scoped on P2P mechanics (no Merlin/ANA tagged). The Merlin intake and autonomous-sourcing story landed as ROI narrative, not as a headline AI pitch.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 5.0 / Moderate with a Neutral stated AI stance; calls confirm the field read - genuine IT-led AI exploration (VP-of-IT roadmap, procurement as pilot) but AI is not a must-have on this P2P scope. Autonomous sourcing/negotiation excited Rafal ('that's a big one for us') without being a decision driver.

items.detail: Economic buyer Oksana (CSCO) has never met Zycus; the mid-July funding pitch is champion-carried and Zycus attendance was declined, leaving no direct recovery channel.
Close date has slipped five times (latest 30 Jul -> 19 Aug on 30 Jun); the funding gate is not until mid-July, then a selection and contracting remain.
A multi-vendor selection is deliberately deferred until after funding; Zycus has no names, pricing, or timing on the competing vendors.
AP team prefers all PO/receipt/invoice matching stays in Oracle; the coexistence design (duplicate PO/receiving into Oracle for 3-way match) is unresolved and deferred to a post-funding AP session.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is live and warm but the buyer-facing thread since 18 Jun is rep-initiated (Steve's 29 Jun Horizon invite, response pending); real momentum depends on the mid-July internal funding pitch.
items: Buyer endorsed the conservative business case on 18 Jun and asked for the deck and per-module pricing.
Last two touches (19 Jun recap, 29 Jun Horizon invite) are Zycus-initiated and awaiting buyer reply.
Champions confirmed the project is 'fully on the P2P train' and 'further cemented' by the restructure.

items.action: Give Jacquelyn and Rafal an executive one-page ROI summary and rehearse the CSCO pitch.
items.trigger: Champions present to CSCO Oksana and VP Procurement mid-July; Zycus attendance declined.
items.expected_effect: Makes the champions unbeatable in a room Zycus cannot enter, protecting the funding decision that gates the whole deal.

items.action: Ask Jacquelyn to name the other vendors in the evaluation.
items.trigger: Jacquelyn: 'we've got multiple vendors that we're looking at.'
items.expected_effect: Turns a blind post-funding bake-off into a targeted displacement plan with a named wedge.

items.action: Send per-module pricing for Scope A versus Scope B with projected savings per module.
items.trigger: Jacquelyn asked for the cost of each module and the return.
items.expected_effect: Removes the last input the champions need to present and lets the deal move the moment funding lands.

items.trigger: AP team prefers matching in Oracle; buyer wants coexistence proof.
items.expected_effect: De-risks the biggest technical objection and pre-stages delivery for the post-funding selection window.

items.trigger: Rebecah, 31 Mar: 'I'm interested in having you guys do a demo... intake to management.'
items.expected_effect: Re-engages the third thread (contract repository owner) and strengthens the Scope B case.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is the front-runner on relationship and a co-built conservative business case, with two champions racing to a mid-July CSCO funding pitch; the only things between us and the win are a room we cannot enter, an unnamed multi-vendor bake-off queued behind funding, and a close date that has already slipped five times.

win_position.text: Win holds around the mid-50s for a Formal Evaluation deal: strong on champions and a buyer-endorsed conservative ROI (Rafal, 18 Jun: 'you basically delivered what we've asked for'), but capped because no economic buyer has been met and a vendor selection is still ahead.
Two engaged champions, Jacquelyn Guerrero and Rafal Prylinski, co-built the business case and will personally present it to the CSCO and VP of Procurement mid-July (Avoma, 18 Jun).

Economic buyer Oksana (CSCO) has never met Zycus and attendance at the funding pitch was declined ('she is most receptive to her internal team'), so there is no recovery channel if the champion-carried pitch underperforms (Avoma, 18 Jun).
A multi-vendor selection is deliberately deferred until after funding and Zycus has no names, pricing, or timing on the competing vendors (Jacquelyn, 18 Jun).

deal_momentum.text: The 18 Jun final business case review closed with the buyer asking for the deck and per-module pricing and naming a concrete mid-July executive presentation (Avoma, 18 Jun).
Both touches since 18 Jun are Zycus-initiated (19 Jun recap, 29 Jun Horizon 2026 invite) and awaiting a buyer reply, so current momentum is rep-side, not buyer-pull.

customer_commitment.text: Jacquelyn confirmed the project is 'fully on the P2P train' and the June restructure 'further cements the need for P2P' (Avoma, 18 Jun).
Commitment stops short of a Zycus decision: the buyer is still weighing Scope A vs Scope B and keeping a competitive selection open after funding.

deal_risk.text: The 19 Aug close is not credible: a mid-July funding gate, then vendor selection, then contracting and Oracle integration cannot complete in 44 days (fifth slip logged 30 Jun).
The AP team's insistence on keeping 3-way matching in Oracle is an unresolved integration objection deferred to a post-funding session (Jacquelyn, 18 Jun).

close_plan_concretizing.evidence: Clear sequenced process (pre-run, mid-July CSCO funding pitch, then selection) but Zycus locked out of the decisive room.

executive_alignment.evidence: Economic buyer Oksana never met; Zycus attendance at the funding pitch declined.

timeline.evidence: Close date slipped five times; funding gate not until mid-July.

scope_change.to: P2P core with an open Scope B (full source-to-pay + iContract/autonomous sourcing) upsell under active consideration
scope_change.detail: Scope has not narrowed; the buyer is weighing whether to expand from P2P to full source-to-pay with contract management, an expansion signal rather than a defensive cut.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer engaged deeply and endorsed the conservative model ('you basically delivered what we've asked for'), but explicitly kept a multi-vendor selection open after funding rather than declaring a Zycus preference.

business_case.evidence: Co-built conservative P2P model: ~8-month payback, 5-7x five-year ROI, one point of managed spend ~$1M, on ~$208M total / ~$86M indirect spend targeting 90% managed.

expansion_context.note: New-business P2P deal; no sibling Closed-Won on Amy's Kitchen, so executive access is not inherited and the unmet CSCO economic buyer is a genuine gap.

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: Activities last 120d; field history to 25 Nov 2025
gaps: Competing vendor names, pricing, and timing are unknown; the bake-off is sequenced after the mid-July funding gate.
Economic buyer Oksana (CSCO) and the VP of Procurement have never been engaged directly by Zycus.
Only two buyer calls exist (31 Mar first draft, 18 Jun final draft); no discovery-era competitive-shortlist call was found.
